courses.title: The Language of Film: Film History
The Language of Film: Theory and Practice

notes: PREPARATION FOR THE MAJOR Although not required for admission, transfer students are encouraged to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: FILM 20A – Introduction to Film Studies ONE lower-division Film and Digital Media course Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" and "Additional Major Preparation Courses" sections below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. A maximum of two courses from another institution may be substituted if articulated through assist.org or approved by Film and Digital Media faculty. At least one of the major qualification courses must be taken at UCSC. FILM 20P cannot be used toward declaring the major. Additional Information for Transfer Transfer students can finish the film and digital media general major requirements within two years. While not required for admission, students are encouraged to complete the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) , or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before matriculation. For more information on incorporating IGETC into your academic plan, please consult with your community college counselor. Integrated Critical Practice Concentration In addition to the general Film and Digital Media major, students may choose to pursue the Integrated Critical Practice Concentration (ICPC) . This concentration offers a more rigorous pathway, tailored for seniors with exceptional abilities in both critical studies and production. It provides exclusive classes designed for those who aim to integrate creative and scholarly work at an advanced level. Please visit the Film & Digital Media Department website to learn more about this major.

All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that ensures your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. Although not required for admission, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division preparatory courses as possible from among those listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section before transferring to UC Santa Cruz. Doing so will help you make timely progress toward degree completion. Additionally, completing the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) is encouraged, especially for non-screening majors in the Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences Divisions. While not required for admission, IGETC completion will facilitate timely graduation. For more information on incorporating IGETC into your academic plan, please consult with your community college counselor.
